summary: Administrative branch-integration PR intended to merge `develop` into `master`, with explicit instruction not to squash and to rebase instead. The PR was closed without being merged, and the only follow-up comment says the author would reintroduce their changes in a different way. No changed files or diff were present in the slice, so there is no evidence of specific code, decompilation, matching, or symbol changes.
what_changed: PR body instructed maintainers: "Do not squash this PR; rebase instead."
The PR was closed without merge.
A later comment stated: "Will reintroduce my changes in a different way."
smart_moves: The PR explicitly documented integration strategy, warning not to squash and preferring rebase; this matters for preserving branch history in a large decompilation repository.
Closing the PR and planning to reintroduce changes differently avoided forcing a potentially unsuitable branch merge into `master`.
decomp_lessons: For broad integration PRs, record whether history should be preserved via rebase rather than squash; this is especially important when many decompilation commits may need traceability.
If a PR is only a branch merge and contains no reviewable diff in the slice, future agents should not infer technical changes from the branch names alone.

review_feedback.feedback: Do not squash this PR; rebase instead.
Will reintroduce my changes in a different way.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs by ribbanya after 2022-12-28 that reintroduced the closed changes from PR #585.
Search for branch integration PRs mentioning "Do not squash" or "rebase instead" to understand repository merge-history policy.
Check whether `develop` to `master` integration happened through a different PR after #585 was closed.
